stem: An isosceles right triangle has a hypotenuse of length 46 inches. What is the perimeter, in inches, of this triangle?
rationale: For an isosceles right triangle, the hypotenuse equals leg · √2, so each leg has length 46/√2 = 23√2. The perimeter equals 2(23√2) + 46 = 46√2 + 46. Choice A is one leg; choice C is the sum of the two legs only; choice D doubles the legs in error.
